Mumbai's drinking water stock has climbed to nearly 70% following heavy monsoon rainfall over the past 24 hours. According to the Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC), the combined storage in the city's seven reservoirs stood at 10,09,432 million litres, or 69.74% of their total capacity, as of 6 am on Thursday.
The water stock increased by 7.84% in the 24 hours, providing a significant boost to the city's water supply.
The seven reservoirs supplying drinking water to Mumbai - Bhatsa, Upper Vaitarna, Middle Vaitarna, Modak Sagar, Tansa, Vihar and Tulsi - together hold 10,09,432 million litres of water against their total capacity of 14,47,363 million litres. Despite the sharp rise, storage remains below the 86.88% recorded during the same period last year.
Four reservoirs overflowing
BMC data shows that Tulsi, Vihar, Tansa and Modak Sagar are currently overflowing. Among the remaining reservoirs, Bhatsa, Mumbai's largest source of drinking water, is at 65.01% of its live storage capacity, Middle Vaitarna at 62.72%, and Upper Vaitarna at 49.41%.
The catchment areas received widespread rainfall during the past 24 hours. Upper Vaitarna recorded 230 mm of rain, followed by Middle Vaitarna (207 mm), Modak Sagar (185 mm), Tulsi (163 mm), Bhatsa (123 mm), Tansa (103 mm) and Vihar (103 mm).
According to the BMC, Modak Sagar began overflowing at 2:07 am on July 23, while Tansa started overflowing on July 22 at 8:51 am. Tulsi and Vihar have been overflowing since July 7.

full_text:
7 inches of rain lash Umargam in two hours:Surat receives 4 inches as two creeks overflow, Intense downpour in Valsad; Red alert for 3 districts
- Copy link
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast heavy rainfall across parts of Gujarat for today (23 July). A red alert has been issued for Panchmahal, Mahisagar and Dahod districts in Central Gujarat, while 11 districts are under an orange alert and 10 districts under a yellow alert.
In Surat, nearly 4 inches of rain was recorded between 4 am and 8 am. Heavy rainfall in the upper catchment areas caused the Bhedwad and Simada creeks flowing through the city to overflow, while several other creeks are also flowing close to the danger mark.
As a precautionary measure, authorities shifted 2,244 residents living near the creeks to safer locations.
Umargam records highest rainfall; over 100 talukas receive showers
Between 6 am and 8 am, rainfall was recorded in 108 talukas across the state. Umargam in Valsad district received the highest rainfall at 7.20 inches, followed by Umarpada in Surat district with 5.15 inches.
Among the lowest rainfall figures, Dolvan recorded 1.30 inches, followed by Vyara with 1.34 inches and Subir with 1.57 inches.
Rescue operations launched as rivers swell in Valsad and Tapi
In Valsad, more than 200 people were shifted to safer locations late at night after the Auranga River rose to the danger level of 9 metres due to heavy rainfall.
In Tapi district, torrential rain led to overflowing streams on the Vyara-Unai National Highway, disrupting traffic as floodwaters swept across the road.
South Gujarat receives highest rainfall; Narmada Dam at 68% capacity
Gujarat has received 31% of its seasonal rainfall so far. Water levels in the state's 206 reservoirs have reached 41% of their total storage capacity, while the Narmada Dam currently stands at 68% capacity.
Among all regions, South Gujarat has recorded the highest rainfall at 50% of its seasonal average, whereas Kutch has received the least, with just 3.5% rainfall so far this monsoon season.
Scenes of rainfall from across Gujarat
Heavy to extremely heavy rain likely to continue till July 26
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast heavy to extremely heavy rainfall across several parts of Gujarat over the next three days.
On July 24, Panchmahal, Dahod and Mahisagar are likely to receive extremely heavy rainfall. Heavy to very heavy rain has also been forecast for Sabarkantha, Gandhinagar, Aravalli, Kheda, Vadodara, Chota Udaipur, Narmada, Dang, Navsari, Valsad, Tapi, Daman and Dadra & Nagar Haveli. Ahmedabad, Anand, Bharuch and Surat are also expected to receive heavy rainfall.
On July 25, extremely heavy rain is expected in Sabarkantha and Aravalli, while Banaskantha, Vav-Tharad, Mehsana, Gandhinagar, Dahod and Mahisagar are likely to witness heavy rainfall.
On July 26, Banaskantha and Sabarkantha are expected to receive extremely heavy rain, with heavy showers forecast for Vav-Tharad, Mehsana and Aravalli.
IMD warns of strong winds; South Gujarat records intense rainfall
The IMD has also forecast thunderstorms accompanied by winds of 30-40 kmph in several parts of the state over the coming days.
During the last 24 hours, parts of South Gujarat, including Valsad, Navsari, Daman and Dadra & Nagar Haveli, recorded extremely heavy rainfall.
Light to moderate rain expected in Ahmedabad
According to IMD meteorologist A.T. Desai, Ahmedabad is expected to receive light to moderate rainfall today. Similar weather conditions are likely to continue in the city over the next two days.
Overflowing creek inundates Parvat village, roads submerged in Surat
Heavy rainfall and the overflowing of a creek triggered flood-like conditions in Surat's Parvat village, leaving the area virtually cut off and surrounded by water. The inundation caused major inconvenience to residents and commuters as knee-deep water submerged roads, severely disrupting movement.
Several vehicles broke down after getting stranded in the floodwaters, forcing motorists to push them to safer locations. The waterlogging also affected daily life, with residents struggling to navigate the flooded streets.
School flooded as creek overflows
Shri Saraswati Hindi Vidyalaya on Parvat Patiya Road was also inundated after the nearby creek overflowed. Around three to four feet of water entered the school premises, disrupting the campus and highlighting the impact of rising water levels in the area.
Purna River crosses danger mark, low-lying areas inundated in Navsari
The water level of the Purna River has risen sharply following widespread rainfall in Navsari district and heavy showers in the upper catchment areas. The river has crossed its danger mark of 23 feet and reached 24 feet, prompting heightened concern among the district administration.
Floodwaters have started entering more than 15 low-lying areas of Navsari city. In response, the Municipal Corporation and district authorities have begun evacuating residents from vulnerable areas and urged people to remain alert.
Those affected are being shifted to schools and other relief shelters, where food and other essential facilities have been arranged. As a precautionary measure, schools and colleges across the district have remained closed since yesterday.
Police rescue stranded residents from flood-hit villages
Police personnel also joined rescue operations in flood-affected areas of Navsari district. Bilimora Police rescued residents trapped in floodwaters at Undach village, carrying them to safety.
Police constable Vikramsinh Thakor was seen carrying a child in his arms while evacuating the family from the flooded area.
Western Railway cancels key trains due to heavy rainfall
Heavy rainfall has disrupted train services on Western Railway's Mumbai Central Division. Train operations were temporarily suspended after the water level rose near Railway Bridge No. 203 between Gholvad and Umargam stations as a safety precaution.
Several important trains scheduled for July 23 have been cancelled, including Train No. 22953 Gujarat Superfast Express from Mumbai Central to Ahmedabad, Train Nos. 20901/20902 Vande Bharat Express operating between Mumbai Central and Gandhinagar Capital, and Train Nos. 12009/12010 Shatabdi Express running between Mumbai Central and Ahmedabad.
Western Railway has advised passengers to check the latest status of their trains before starting their journey and to follow all travel advisories and updates issued by the railway authorities.
Torrential rain floods roads in Vyara, commuters face disruption
Overnight torrential rain inundated roads in the Kanpura area of Vyara town in Tapi district, causing major inconvenience to motorists and residents. Waterlogging on key roads disrupted traffic movement, with commuters struggling to navigate flooded stretches.
Residents expressed anger over the recurring flooding, alleging that despite repeated incidents every monsoon, the municipal authorities have failed to develop an effective drainage system to prevent waterlogging.
Lake overflows near Vyara's City Mall, recurring flooding sparks public anger
Heavy rainfall in Vyara also caused the town's central lake to overflow, with water spilling onto roads near City Mall and creating severe inconvenience for residents and commuters.
Locals said the area faces flooding every monsoon and demanded a permanent solution from the Vyara Municipality. Residents urged civic authorities to take concrete measures to prevent the annual recurrence of waterlogging around the City Mall area.
Vijaynagar records highest rainfall in Sabarkantha
Sabarkantha district witnessed widespread rainfall over the past 24 hours, with Vijaynagar recording the highest rainfall at 42 mm. Other rainfall figures included 17 mm in Poshina, 15 mm in Khedbrahma, 12 mm in Himmatnagar, 10 mm each in Prantij and Talod, 3 mm in Idar and 2 mm in Vadali.
Rain-bearing clouds continued to hover over Himmatnagar and other parts of the district on Thursday morning after showers across the district a day earlier. The rainfall has brought relief to farmers by benefiting kharif crops such as groundnut and cotton, while the drop in temperature has also provided respite from the recent heat and humidity.

title: Safe Room to open Monday at 10 a.m. for cooling shelter for another extreme heat event - The Natchez Democrat

full_text:
NATCHEZ — More heat, perhaps even hotter than the extreme heat the Miss-Lou has experience this week, is expected to arrive on Monday.
Because of that, the Adams County Safe Room will be open on Monday, Tuesday and Wednesday as a cooling shelter during the day, said Capt. Tom McGehee of the Adams County Emergency Management Agency.
The National Weather Service in Jackson is calling for the high temperature on Monday to be 99 degrees and Tuesday to be 100 degrees, without consideration of any heat indices.
"The Safe Room will open as a Cool Room, Monday at 10 a.m. to close at about 5 p.m.," McGehee said. "Bring your medication and kennel for your pets. Access will be available to the refrigerator, ice machine and tap water."
The Safe Room will be open also Tuesday and Wednesday, he said.
"We are expecting Extreme Heat Warnings those days and may get into Excessive Heat Warnings," McGehee said.
